tools.name: network.cli.exec
tools.description: Execute operational CLI show commands on the network device. Used for retrieving operational state, statistics, and diagnostic information. Commands run in operational mode and cannot modify configuration.

cmd.description: The CLI command to execute (e.g., 'show interfaces', 'show ip route vrf mgmt')

tools.name: network.cli.configure
tools.description: Enter configuration mode and send configuration commands. Commands are applied to the candidate datastore and require a commit operation to take effect. Supports both single commands and multi-line configuration blocks.

commands.description: Array of configuration commands to execute in order

tools.name: network.yang.get
tools.description: Retrieve YANG data node from the specified datastore. Returns structured data conforming to the YANG model. Supports XPath-style paths for precise data selection.

depth.description: Maximum depth of subtree to retrieve (0 = unlimited)

tools.name: network.yang.edit
tools.description: Edit the candidate datastore using YANG data structures. Supports create, merge, replace, and delete operations. Changes are staged in candidate datastore until committed.

value.description: YANG data to apply (omit for delete operations)

tools.name: network.commit
tools.description: Commit candidate configuration to running datastore. Supports confirmed commit with automatic rollback if not confirmed within timeout period. Returns commit-id for tracking.

confirmed.description: Confirmed commit timeout in seconds (0 = normal commit)

comment.description: Optional commit comment for audit trail

tools.name: network.rollback
tools.description: Rollback configuration to a previous commit. Can rollback to a specific commit-id or to the last committed state. Only available if supportsRollback capability is true.

commitId.description: Specific commit ID to rollback to (optional, defaults to previous commit)

tools.name: network.file.pull
tools.description: Backup a configuration file from the device to the controller. Supports running-config, startup-config, and other device files. Returns file content as base64-encoded string for binary files.

tools.name: network.file.push
tools.description: Restore a configuration file to the device. File content is validated before being written to the specified target. Supports atomic replace operations.

resources.name: All Interfaces
resources.description: Complete list of all network interfaces with operational and configuration state. Supports cursor-based pagination for large interface counts.

resources.name: IPv4 Routing Table
resources.description: Complete IPv4 routing information base (RIB) from operational datastore. Includes all routes with next-hop, metric, and protocol information.

resources.name: IPv6 Routing Table
resources.description: Complete IPv6 routing information base (RIB) from operational datastore. Includes all routes with next-hop, metric, and protocol information.

resources.name: CPU Utilization
resources.description: Real-time CPU utilization percentage across all cores. Updated every 30 seconds. Supports subscription for continuous monitoring.

resources.name: Memory Summary
resources.description: Memory usage summary including total, used, free, and cached memory. Updated every 30 seconds.

resources.name: Running Configuration
resources.description: Current active configuration as plain text. This is the live configuration that determines device behavior.

resources.name: Startup Configuration
resources.description: Configuration loaded on device boot. This may differ from running-config if changes have not been saved.

resources.name: Candidate Configuration
resources.description: Staged configuration changes that have not yet been committed. This datastore is used for two-phase commit operations.

resourceTemplates.name: Single Interface
resourceTemplates.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific interface by name. Returns YANG-modeled data including operational state, statistics, and configuration. Supports both OpenConfig and IETF interface models.

resourceTemplates.name: Recent Syslog Entries
resourceTemplates.description: Retrieve the most recent syslog messages from the device. Maximum 10000 entries. Returns plain text with one message per line including timestamp and severity.

count.description: Number of recent syslog entries to retrieve (1-10000)

resourceTemplates.name: Crash Information
resourceTemplates.description: Retrieve crash information file for a specific hardware slot. Returns JSON array of crash records with timestamp, reason, and stack trace.

prompts.name: network.troubleshoot.ping-fail
prompts.description: Step-by-step diagnostic workflow for ping failure scenarios. Guides through ARP verification, interface status checks, routing table inspection, and extended ping tests. Returns actionable remediation steps.

arguments.name: src
arguments.description: Source IP address for ping test

arguments.name: dst
arguments.description: Destination IP address that is unreachable

arguments.name: vrf
arguments.description: VRF/routing instance name (optional, default is global routing table)

prompts.name: network.config.add-vlan
prompts.description: Interactive VLAN creation wizard. Guides through VLAN ID selection, naming, and interface assignment. Validates VLAN ID ranges and generates appropriate configuration commands for the device CLI dialect.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: VLAN name (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores only - no spaces)

arguments.name: ports
arguments.description: List of interface names to add to the VLAN (optional)

prompts.name: network.security.audit
prompts.description: Generate and execute security compliance checks based on predefined profiles. Returns detailed audit report with pass/fail status, findings, and remediation recommendations. Supports basic and detailed audit profiles.

runtimeObservations.instructions: MCP server for network equipment management. Supports YANG data models, CLI commands, configuration management with confirmed commits, and real-time monitoring. See IETF draft-zw-opsawg-mcp-network-mgmt-00 for full specification.
